title: [InstCombine] Propagate FMF from fptrunc when folding `fptrunc fabs(X) -> fabs(fptrunc X)`
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by recognizing that `fptrunc` can safely propagate its own fast-math flags (FMF) when sinking it through an intrinsic like `llvm.fabs`, rather than copying flags from the intrinsic. This is because `fptrunc` may create infinities from normal values, so `nnan` and `nsz` from `fptrunc` are safe, but `ninf` from the intrinsic cannot be preserved.

The generalization extends the pattern from a single intrinsic (`llvm.fabs`) to any FP unary intrinsic where the same flag propagation logic applies. It also broadens from `fast` to specific flags like `nsz`, as shown in the new test cases where `nsz` on a select-based fabs pattern allows the same transformation.

**Key insight**: When sinking a cast through an operation, the cast's FMF constraints (regarding rounding and overflow) dominate; intrinsic flags are only partially preserved. This approach can be applied to other cast+intrinsic combinations where the cast may introduce new FP exceptions.
